rationale: The only resume change was employer location (Berlin/Cluj-Napoca/etc. to United States), which carries no job-relevant information. Both evaluations award an identical 8/10. The wording differences — '15+ years' vs '10+ years', and new concerns about PostgreSQL depth and Kubernetes-at-scale leadership — are not anchored to the geographic signal; they read as ordinary run-to-run rephrasing and reshuffling of the same underlying gaps (Nest.js, Vitest, Kotlin/Java recency). No concern, score change, or reasoning shift keys off the candidate being located in the United States versus Europe.
